# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
def index
  search = params[:search]
  product_query_hash = {}

  %w[name category price min max].each do |column|
    if search["#{column}_greater"].present?
      search["price"] << " and #{column} > '#{search['#{column}']}"
    end

    if search["#{column}_less"].present?
       search["price"] << " and #{column} < '#{search['#{column}']}"
     end
  end

  # product_query_hash[:where] = " ( (name LIKE '%#{search[:name]}%' or category like '%#{search[:category]}%') ) AND (price > #{search[:min].to_i} OR price < #{search[:max].to_i}) " if search.present?
  # product_query_hash[:where] = " ( (name LIKE '%#{search[:name]}%' or category like '%#{search[:category]}%') ) AND (price > #{search[:min].to_i} OR price < #{search[:max].to_i}) " if search.present?

  product_query_hash[:where] = search['product']
  product_query_hash[:select] = "products.*"

@products = Product.find(:all, :conditions => [product_query_hash])



end
